title: Essential Math Primer
description: 15-minute standalone lessons covering K-12 math concepts

lessons.title: What Numbers Are
lessons.description: Counting, place value foundation, number sense

lessons.title: Addition Basics
lessons.description: Strategies, mental math, building fluency

lessons.title: Subtraction & Inverse Relationships
lessons.description: Subtraction as inverse of addition, conceptual understanding

lessons.title: Multiplication Concept
lessons.description: Arrays, repeated addition, foundational multiplication

lessons.title: Division & Fair Sharing
lessons.description: Division as inverse of multiplication, fair sharing concept

lessons.title: Fractions as Parts
lessons.description: Halves, thirds, fourths, intuitive fraction understanding

lessons.title: Comparing & Ordering
lessons.description: Comparing numbers and fractions, ordering on number line

lessons.title: Introduction to Measurement
lessons.description: Length, time, money, standard units

lessons.title: Basic Geometry
lessons.description: Shapes, properties, perimeter, area basics

lessons.title: Word Problems Strategy
lessons.description: Translating English to math, problem-solving approach

lessons.title: Patterns & Sequences
lessons.description: Identifying and extending patterns, sequence basics

lessons.title: Data & Basic Graphing
lessons.description: Collecting data, pictographs, bar graphs, interpretation

lessons.title: Place Value: Tens and Ones
lessons.description: Students explore how two-digit numbers are built from groups of ten and leftover ones using base-10 blocks and place value charts. They learn to decompose numbers like 47 into 4 tens and 7 ones, read and write two-digit numbers in expanded form, and understand that the position of a digit determines its value.

lessons.title: Hundreds and Regrouping: Building Bigger Numbers
lessons.description: Students extend place value understanding to three-digit numbers by discovering the hundreds place. Using base-10 blocks, they see that 10 tens make 1 hundred, compose and decompose numbers like 365 into 3 hundreds, 6 tens, and 5 ones, and practice regrouping — trading 10 ones for 1 ten or 10 tens for 1 hundred.

lessons.title: Comparing and Ordering Numbers
lessons.description: Students learn to compare two- and three-digit numbers using place value reasoning. Starting with base-10 block comparisons, they progress to using greater than, less than, and equal to symbols. They practice ordering sets of numbers from least to greatest and greatest to least, and locate numbers on a number line to build number sense.
key_concepts: greater than, less than, equal to symbols
comparing by place value from left to right
ordering numbers on a number line
reasoning about magnitude using hundreds, tens, and ones

lessons.title: Multi-Digit Addition with Regrouping
lessons.description: Students learn to add two- and three-digit numbers using the standard algorithm, with a focus on regrouping (carrying). They begin with base-10 block models to see why 10 ones become 1 ten, then transition to the written vertical method. Problems progress from two-digit plus two-digit with one regrouping to three-digit problems with multiple regroupings.
key_concepts: regrouping ones into tens when adding
regrouping tens into hundreds when adding

lessons.title: Multi-Digit Subtraction with Regrouping
lessons.description: Students learn to subtract two- and three-digit numbers using the standard algorithm, with a focus on regrouping (borrowing). Using base-10 blocks, they see why 1 ten can be broken into 10 ones when there aren't enough ones to subtract. They practice the written vertical method and use addition to check their answers, reinforcing the inverse relationship.
key_concepts: regrouping a ten into ones when subtracting
regrouping a hundred into tens when subtracting

lessons.title: Time and Money in Everyday Life
lessons.description: Students learn to tell time on analog and digital clocks to the hour and half hour, then to five-minute intervals. They explore calendar concepts including days of the week, months, and reading a date. For money, they identify coins and bills, learn their values, and count collections of mixed coins using skip counting. Real-world scenarios connect both skills to daily routines.
key_concepts: reading analog and digital clocks to five-minute intervals
days, weeks, months, and reading calendar dates
counting mixed collections of coins using skip counting

lessons.title: Place Value to Millions & Billions
lessons.description: Extends place value understanding from hundreds to millions and billions. Students explore how each place is ten times the value of the place to its right, read and write large numbers in standard, expanded, and word form, and compare and order numbers up to the billions place.
key_concepts: reading and writing numbers to billions

lessons.title: Multi-Digit Multiplication Strategies
lessons.description: Builds on the multiplication concept to multiply multi-digit numbers using area models, partial products, and the standard algorithm. Students decompose factors by place value, compute partial products, and combine results, progressing from 2-digit x 1-digit to 2-digit x 2-digit multiplication.

lessons.title: Long Division Algorithm
lessons.description: Develops the long division algorithm step by step, connecting division to repeated subtraction and the inverse of multiplication. Students learn the divide-multiply-subtract-bring down cycle, progressing from 2-digit / 1-digit to 4-digit / 1-digit, and interpret remainders in context.

lessons.title: Mixed Numbers & Improper Fractions
lessons.description: Introduces the relationship between mixed numbers and improper fractions. Students use visual models (fraction strips, number lines) to convert between the two forms, compare mixed numbers, and place them on a number line, building the foundation for fraction computation with values greater than one.
key_concepts: converting improper fractions to mixed numbers
converting mixed numbers to improper fractions
placing mixed numbers on a number line

lessons.title: Fraction Operations with Mixed Numbers
lessons.description: Extends addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of fractions to include mixed numbers. Students learn to add and subtract mixed numbers with regrouping, multiply a whole number by a mixed number, and solve real-world problems involving fractional quantities greater than one.
key_concepts: multiplying whole numbers by mixed numbers
solving word problems with mixed number operations

lessons.title: Decimal Operations: Addition & Subtraction
lessons.description: Teaches addition and subtraction of decimal numbers by emphasizing place value alignment. Students use base-ten blocks and hundredths grids to model decimal sums and differences, learn to align decimal points before computing, and estimate to check reasonableness of results.
key_concepts: aligning decimal points for addition and subtraction
modeling decimal operations with base-ten blocks
adding and subtracting decimals through hundredths

lessons.title: Decimal Operations: Multiplication & Division
lessons.description: Extends multiplication and division to decimal numbers. Students explore why multiplying by decimals less than one produces a smaller product, use area models and place value reasoning to multiply decimals, and divide decimals by whole numbers, connecting quotient placement to place value understanding.
key_concepts: multiplying decimals using place value and area models
placing the decimal point in products
estimating to check decimal computation results

lessons.title: Area, Perimeter & Volume Applications
lessons.description: Applies area and perimeter to composite figures by decomposing them into rectangles, and introduces volume as the space inside a three-dimensional solid. Students calculate area of L-shapes and composite rectangles, solve real-world perimeter problems, and find volume of rectangular prisms by counting unit cubes and using the length x width x height formula.
key_concepts: area of composite figures by decomposition
volume as counting unit cubes in a rectangular prism
volume formula: length x width x height

lessons.title: Equivalent Fractions & Simplification
lessons.description: Equivalent fractions, reducing to lowest terms

lessons.title: Adding & Subtracting Fractions
lessons.description: Common denominators, operations with fractions

lessons.title: Multiplying & Dividing Fractions
lessons.description: Multiplication and division algorithms for fractions

lessons.title: Decimals & Place Value
lessons.description: Decimal notation, operations, relationship to fractions

lessons.title: Percentages
lessons.description: Percent concept, conversions, calculations

lessons.title: Ratios & Proportional Reasoning
lessons.description: Ratios, rates, proportions, direct relationships

lessons.title: Integers & Operations with Negatives
lessons.description: Negative numbers, integer operations, number line

lessons.title: Introduction to Variables
lessons.description: Variables as unknown quantities, basic notation

lessons.title: Solving Simple Equations
lessons.description: Two-step equations, inverse operations, balance method

lessons.title: Order of Operations
lessons.description: PEMDAS, sequence of operations, evaluation

lessons.title: Exponents & Powers
lessons.description: Exponential notation, properties of exponents, powers of 10

lessons.title: Square Roots & Radicals
lessons.description: Square roots, radical notation, perfect squares intro

lessons.title: Graphing Ordered Pairs
lessons.description: Coordinate plane, ordered pairs, plotting points

lessons.title: Introduction to Functions
lessons.description: Function concept, input-output, function notation

lessons.title: Linear Relationships
lessons.description: Linear patterns, constant rate, introduction to linear functions

lessons.title: Angles & Angle Relationships
lessons.description: Introduces angle measurement in degrees, classifies angles as acute, right, obtuse, and straight, and explores key angle relationships including complementary, supplementary, vertical, and adjacent angles. Students learn to use these relationships to find unknown angle measures.

lessons.title: Parallel Lines & Transversals
lessons.description: Explores what happens when a transversal crosses parallel lines, identifying corresponding, alternate interior, alternate exterior, and co-interior (same-side interior) angle pairs. Students apply angle relationships to determine unknown measures and reason about why lines must be parallel.
key_concepts: alternate interior and alternate exterior angles
co-interior angles are supplementary (sum to 180 degrees)

lessons.title: Triangles: Classification, Angle Sum & Congruence
lessons.description: Classifies triangles by side length (scalene, isosceles, equilateral) and by angle measure (acute, right, obtuse). Establishes the triangle angle-sum property (interior angles sum to 180 degrees) and introduces triangle congruence criteria (SSS, SAS, ASA) through hands-on reasoning.
key_concepts: classifying triangles by sides and angles

lessons.title: Geometric Transformations
lessons.description: Introduces rigid transformations (translations, reflections, rotations) and non-rigid transformations (dilations) on the coordinate plane. Students describe transformations using precise language and coordinates, verify that rigid transformations preserve size and shape, and explore how dilations change size while preserving shape.
key_concepts: dilations with scale factor from a center point

lessons.title: Circles: Circumference, Area & Pi
lessons.description: Explores the relationship between a circle's diameter and circumference to discover pi. Develops and applies the formulas for circumference (C = pi*d = 2*pi*r) and area (A = pi*r^2). Students solve real-world problems involving circular measurements.
key_concepts: pi as the ratio of circumference to diameter

lessons.title: Volume & Surface Area of 3D Solids
lessons.description: Extends 2D area concepts to three dimensions. Students learn to calculate the volume and surface area of prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres. Emphasizes understanding formulas through net diagrams and cross-sectional reasoning rather than pure memorization.
key_concepts: volume of prisms and cylinders (V = Bh)
volume of pyramids, cones (V = 1/3 Bh), and spheres (V = 4/3 pi r^3)
surface area using nets and formulas

lessons.title: Data Displays & Measures of Center and Spread
lessons.description: Builds on basic graphing to introduce histograms, dot plots, and box plots for displaying distributions of numerical data. Students calculate and interpret mean, median, mode, range, interquartile range (IQR), and mean absolute deviation (MAD), choosing appropriate measures for symmetric versus skewed distributions.
key_concepts: histograms and dot plots for numerical data
mean, median, and mode as measures of center
range, IQR, and mean absolute deviation as measures of spread
effect of outliers and skewness on center and spread

lessons.title: Probability: Sample Spaces & Experimental vs Theoretical
lessons.description: Introduces probability as a measure from 0 to 1 representing likelihood. Students list sample spaces for simple and compound events, calculate theoretical probability using equally likely outcomes, conduct experiments to find experimental (relative frequency) probability, and compare the two as trial counts increase (law of large numbers intuition).
key_concepts: sample spaces for simple and compound events
theoretical probability as favorable outcomes over total outcomes
experimental probability and the law of large numbers

lessons.title: Linear Equations
lessons.description: Slope-intercept, point-slope, standard form

lessons.title: Graphing Linear Functions
lessons.description: Graphing lines, slope interpretation, transformations

lessons.title: Systems of Linear Equations
lessons.description: Solving systems, graphical and algebraic methods

lessons.title: Slope & Rate of Change
lessons.description: Slope concept, rate of change, secant lines

lessons.title: Polynomials & Terminology
lessons.description: Polynomial definitions, degree, standard form

lessons.title: Factoring Techniques
lessons.description: GCF, trinomials, special products, factoring

lessons.title: Quadratic Equations - Conceptual
lessons.description: Quadratic concept, parabolas, roots, vertex

lessons.title: Solving Quadratics
lessons.description: Factoring, quadratic formula, completing the square

lessons.title: Function Notation & Composition
lessons.description: Function notation, evaluation, composition of functions

lessons.title: Inverse Functions
lessons.description: Inverse concept, finding inverses, properties

lessons.title: Exponential Functions & Growth
lessons.description: Exponential models, exponential growth/decay, e

lessons.title: Logarithms
lessons.description: Logarithms as inverse of exponentials, properties, evaluation

lessons.title: Rational Expressions & Equations
lessons.description: Simplifying, operations, solving rational equations

lessons.title: Sequences - Arithmetic
lessons.description: Arithmetic sequences, common difference, formulas

lessons.title: Sequences - Geometric
lessons.description: Geometric sequences, common ratio, formulas

lessons.title: Right Triangle Trigonometry
lessons.description: Sine, cosine, tangent, right triangle applications

lessons.title: Unit Circle & Angle Measure
lessons.description: Unit circle, radians, degrees, angle measurement

lessons.title: Basic Trigonometric Functions
lessons.description: Sine, cosine, tangent as functions, graphs, properties

lessons.title: Probability Fundamentals
lessons.description: Probability rules, independent/dependent events, counting

lessons.title: Statistics & Distributions
lessons.description: Mean, median, mode, standard deviation, normal distribution

lessons.title: Correlation vs. Causation
lessons.description: Relationship analysis, correlation coefficient, inference

lessons.title: Geometric Proof Foundations
lessons.description: Introduction to formal geometric reasoning: axioms, postulates, and proof structures. Covers direct proof, proof by contradiction, and two-column proof format using properties of equality, congruence, and basic angle relationships (vertical angles, supplementary angles, corresponding angles).
key_concepts: vertical angles, linear pairs, and parallel line angle theorems

lessons.title: Coordinate Geometry Proofs
lessons.description: Using the coordinate plane to prove geometric properties algebraically. Covers midpoint formula, slope criteria for parallel and perpendicular lines, and distance formula applications to prove that figures are parallelograms, rectangles, rhombi, or other specific quadrilaterals by verifying side lengths, slopes, and diagonal properties.
key_concepts: midpoint formula and segment bisector proofs
slope criteria for parallel and perpendicular lines
proving quadrilateral classifications on the coordinate plane

lessons.title: Similarity and Proportionality
lessons.description: Establishing triangle similarity through AA, SAS, and SSS similarity criteria. Covers ratio and proportion in similar figures, the Triangle Proportionality Theorem (a line parallel to one side of a triangle divides the other two sides proportionally), geometric mean, and applications to indirect measurement.
key_concepts: AA, SAS, and SSS similarity criteria
scale factor and proportional reasoning in similar figures

lessons.title: Circle Theorems
lessons.description: Key theorems relating arcs, chords, inscribed angles, and tangent lines in circles. Covers the Inscribed Angle Theorem (an inscribed angle is half its intercepted arc), Thales' theorem (inscribed angle in a semicircle is 90 degrees), tangent-radius perpendicularity, and the two-tangent theorem.
key_concepts: Inscribed Angle Theorem and its corollaries
arc, chord, and central angle relationships

lessons.title: Polynomial Functions Beyond Quadratics
lessons.description: Analyzing cubic, quartic, and higher-degree polynomial functions. Covers end behavior determined by leading term, finding zeros using the Rational Root Theorem and synthetic division, multiplicity of roots and their effect on graph behavior (crossing vs tangent at x-axis), and the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra.
key_concepts: end behavior and leading coefficient test
Rational Root Theorem and synthetic division
root multiplicity and graph behavior at zeros

lessons.title: Piecewise and Absolute Value Functions
lessons.description: Defining and graphing functions with different rules over different intervals. Covers piecewise function notation, evaluating and graphing piecewise-defined functions, continuity at boundary points, and absolute value functions as a special case of piecewise. Includes transformations of absolute value functions and solving absolute value equations and inequalities.
key_concepts: graphing piecewise functions and checking continuity at boundaries
absolute value as a piecewise function

lessons.title: Introduction to Limits
lessons.description: Building intuitive understanding of limits as the foundation of calculus. Covers the informal definition of a limit (the value a function approaches as x approaches a given value), evaluating limits from tables and graphs, one-sided limits, limits at infinity and horizontal asymptotes, and cases where limits do not exist.
key_concepts: informal definition of limit and limit notation
one-sided limits and conditions for limit existence
limits at infinity and connection to horizontal asymptotes

lessons.title: Parametric Equations and Polar Coordinates Introduction
lessons.description: Representing curves and points using alternative coordinate systems. Covers parametric equations: defining x(t) and y(t) to trace curves (lines, circles, projectile paths), eliminating the parameter to find Cartesian equations. Introduces polar coordinates (r, theta), converting between polar and Cartesian forms, and graphing basic polar curves.
key_concepts: parametric equations for lines, circles, and projectile motion
eliminating the parameter to convert to Cartesian form
polar coordinate system and polar-Cartesian conversion

lessons.title: Scientific Notation
lessons.description: Writing very large and small numbers efficiently using powers of 10

lessons.title: Introduction to Inequalities
lessons.description: Understanding inequality symbols, solving one-step inequalities, number line representation

lessons.title: Percent Applications
lessons.description: Tax, tip, discount, and markup calculations in real-world contexts

lessons.title: Why Division by Zero is Undefined
lessons.description: Understanding why dividing by zero breaks mathematics and what 'undefined' means

lessons.title: Why We 'Flip and Multiply' for Fraction Division
lessons.description: The conceptual reason behind the reciprocal rule for dividing fractions

lessons.title: Distance Formula
lessons.description: Finding distance between two points using coordinates and the Pythagorean theorem

lessons.title: Combining Like Terms & Distributive Property
lessons.description: Simplifying expressions by combining terms and distributing multiplication

lessons.title: Simple Interest
lessons.description: Calculating interest using I = PRT formula for loans and savings

lessons.title: Compound Interest
lessons.description: Understanding compound interest and exponential growth in finance

lessons.title: PEMDAS Pitfalls
lessons.description: Common mistakes with order of operations and left-to-right evaluation

lessons.title: Absolute Value Equations
lessons.description: Solving equations with absolute value and understanding distance interpretation

lessons.title: Line of Best Fit & Linear Regression
lessons.description: Finding trend lines for scatter plot data and making predictions

lessons.title: Combinations & Permutations
lessons.description: Counting principles for ordered and unordered arrangements

lessons.title: Conditional Probability
lessons.description: Probability of events given that another event has occurred

lessons.title: Mean vs Median: When to Use Which
lessons.description: Choosing appropriate measures of center based on data distribution

lessons.title: Function Transformations
lessons.description: Shifts, stretches, reflections, and compressions of function graphs

lessons.title: Rational Functions & Asymptotes
lessons.description: Understanding functions with polynomial ratios and their asymptotic behavior

lessons.title: Rational Exponents
lessons.description: Connecting fractional exponents to radicals and root operations

lessons.title: Introduction to Complex Numbers
lessons.description: The imaginary unit i, complex number operations, and why they exist

lessons.title: Law of Sines
lessons.description: Solving non-right triangles using sine ratios and side-angle relationships

lessons.title: Law of Cosines
lessons.description: Using the generalized Pythagorean theorem for any triangle

lessons.title: Arc Length & Sector Area
lessons.description: Calculating arc lengths and areas of circle sectors using radians

lessons.title: Sigma Notation & Finite Series
lessons.description: Summation notation and finding sums of arithmetic and geometric series

lessons.title: Estimation & Reasonableness
lessons.description: Developing number sense to check if answers make sense in context

lessons.title: Box Plots & Interquartile Range
lessons.description: Five-number summary, box-and-whisker plots, and measuring spread with IQR

lessons.title: Area vs Perimeter Confusion
lessons.description: Common mistakes mixing up area and perimeter and when to use each

lessons.title: Community Choice Topic
lessons.description: Reserved for highest-requested topic from community feedback
